text: Designing good REST APIs makes life easier for developers who use them. Endpoints should be clear and logical, using nouns like `/users` or `/orders` instead of verbs. Status codes are important: `200` means success, `404` means not found, and `500` indicates a server error. Always keep documentation up to date so users know how to work with your API. Also, use versioning, for example `/v1/users`, so changes don't break existing applications. With clear endpoints, proper status codes, good docs, and versioning, your API will be simple and reliable to use.
